Ancient Egyptian.description: An ancient polytheistic religion and mythology of Northeast Africa, centered around deities such as Ra, Osiris, and Isis, with a strong focus on the afterlife.

Anglo-Saxon.description: The Germanic paganism and subsequent heroic folklore practiced by the early medieval inhabitants of England before their conversion to Christianity.

Australian.description: The diverse folklore, myths, and legends recorded on the Australian continent, frequently intersecting with the oral traditions of its Indigenous peoples.

Australian Aboriginal.description: The deeply rooted spiritual belief system of Indigenous Australians, characterized by animism and a mythological era known as the Dreamtime.

Babylonian.description: The mythology and religious practices of ancient Mesopotamia, featuring a pantheon headed by Marduk and epic creation narratives.
books: The Babylonian Legends of the Creation

Buddhism.description: A major world religion and philosophy originating in ancient India, based on the teachings of Siddhartha Gautama (the Buddha), emphasizing the path to enlightenment.

Celtic.description: The polytheistic religion and animistic mythology of the ancient Celtic peoples, primarily preserved in the traditions of Ireland, Scotland, and Wales.

Chinese.description: A complex collection of cultural history, folktales, and ancient religious beliefs from China, integrating natural forces, ancestor worship, and mythological figures.

Christianity.description: An Abrahamic monotheistic religion based on the life, teachings, and resurrection of Jesus of Nazareth.
books: The King James Version of the Bible

Confucianism.description: An ancient Chinese philosophical and ethical system formulated by Confucius, focusing on morality, family hierarchy, and social harmony.

Finnish.description: The traditional pagan religion and mythological folklore of Finland, characterized by shamanism, nature spirits, and epic poetry.
books: Kalevala The Epic Poem of Finland

Germanic.description: The historical religion and mythology of the Germanic peoples, encompassing heroic sagas and a pantheon of gods that influenced Northern and Central Europe.

Greek.description: The body of myths and religious teachings belonging to the ancient Greeks, concerning their polytheistic pantheon of gods, heroes, and the origins of the world.

Hinduism.description: One of the world's oldest religions, originating in the Indian subcontinent, characterized by a diverse range of philosophies, a vast pantheon of deities, and concepts like karma and dharma.

Islam.description: An Abrahamic monotheistic religion articulating that there is only one God (Allah), and that Muhammad is His messenger, centered around the Quran.

Japanese Buddhism.description: The adaptation and practice of Buddhism in Japan, characterized by unique sects like Zen and Pure Land, and its historical syncretism with native Shinto beliefs.

Maori.description: The rich mythological and cultural traditions of the indigenous Polynesian people of New Zealand, featuring tales of creation, demigods like Maui, and ancestral spirits.

Maya.description: The Mesoamerican mythology of the Maya civilization, heavily intertwined with precise astronomy, natural cycles, and a complex pantheon of deities.

Norse.description: The mythology of the North Germanic peoples, derived from Norse paganism, featuring gods such as Odin and Thor, and the cosmological framework of Yggdrasil.

Polynesian.description: The oral traditions, folklore, and mythological narratives shared by the peoples of the Polynesian triangle in the Pacific Ocean.
books: Legends of Ma-ui a Demi God of Polynesia

Roman.description: The mythology and religious system of ancient Rome, heavily influenced by Greek tradition but uniquely focused on statehood, historical foundations, and civic duty.

Taoism.description: An ancient Chinese philosophical and spiritual tradition emphasizing living in harmony with the Tao (the Way), focusing on balance, naturalness, and non-action (wu wei).

West African.description: The diverse and rich collection of traditional religions, folklore, and mythologies belonging to the various ethnic groups of West Africa, often featuring trickster spirits and complex pantheons.
